note: Trace events were declared by the effective adapter generated from source test evidence.

operation_metadata.description: Parse handling of null, empty, and unterminated inputs from source parse_with_opts tests
operation_metadata.normalization: json::parse returns Err for empty or malformed input (source asserts NULL)

input.case: parse_with_opts with NULL (simulated via empty string) and incomplete JSON

expected.observable_behavior: json::parse returns Err for empty and unterminated input

operation_metadata.description: Parse handling of null, empty, and unterminated inputs from source parse_with_opts tests
operation_metadata.normalization: json::parse returns Err for empty or malformed input (source asserts NULL)

input.case: empty string

expected.observable_behavior: json::parse of empty string returns Err

operation_metadata.description: Parse handling of null, empty, and unterminated inputs from source parse_with_opts tests
operation_metadata.normalization: json::parse returns Err for empty or malformed input (source asserts NULL)

input.case: '{}x' with null-termination requirement

expected.observable_behavior: json::parse returns Err for trailing data

operation_metadata.description: Create object and add boolean values, then retrieve by key
operation_metadata.normalization: JsonValue::new_object creates object, JsonValue::insert adds key/value, Object::get retrieves value

input.case: create object, add bool true and false

expected.observable_behavior: retrieved keys are boolean true and false

operation_metadata.description: Create object and add number, then retrieve by key
operation_metadata.normalization: JsonValue::insert with f64 value, Object::get retrieves number

input.case: create object, add number 42

expected.observable_behavior: retrieved key is number with value 42

operation_metadata.description: Create object and add string, then retrieve by key
operation_metadata.normalization: JsonValue::insert with string, Object::get retrieves string

input.case: create object, add string 'Hello World!'

expected.observable_behavior: retrieved key is string 'Hello World!'

operation_metadata.description: Create object and add true/false constants, then retrieve by key
operation_metadata.normalization: JsonValue::insert with Boolean(true) and Boolean(false); Object::get retrieves them

input.case: create object, add true and false

expected.observable_behavior: retrieved keys are boolean true and false

operation_metadata.description: Create object and add empty array and empty object, then retrieve by key
operation_metadata.normalization: JsonValue::insert with new_array() and new_object(); Object::get retrieves them

input.case: create object, add empty array and empty object

expected.observable_behavior: retrieved keys are array and object

operation_metadata.description: Create array, add items, detach first item, check length
operation_metadata.normalization: JsonValue::new_array creates array, push adds elements, array_remove removes and returns removed element, len returns size

input.case: create array, push number, detach first element

expected.observable_behavior: array length decreases and removed element is correct

operation_metadata.description: Parse a JSON object, add array with items, delete an item, verify output with dump
operation_metadata.normalization: Parse '{}', add 'rd' array, push two items, delete first, dump results must match source expected strings

input.case: parse '{}', add array 'rd', push items, delete first, dump

expected.observable_behavior: dump matches expected strings after each mutation

operation_metadata.description: Get string value from string and number values
operation_metadata.normalization: as_str returns Some for string, None for number

input.case: call as_str on string and number values

expected.observable_behavior: as_str returns Some for string, None for number

operation_metadata.description: Get number value from number and string values
operation_metadata.normalization: as_f64 returns Some for number, None for string

input.case: call as_f64 on number and string values

expected.observable_behavior: as_f64 returns Some for number, None for string

operation_metadata.description: Type checking functions for all JSON value types
operation_metadata.normalization: Each is_* returns true only for matching type

input.case: create values of each type and test is_* methods

expected.observable_behavior: each is_* returns true only for matching type

operation_metadata.description: Add item to object then replace it preserving key
operation_metadata.normalization: Insert replaces existing value for same key; Object::get retrieves the new value

input.case: add item then replace, check key preserved

expected.observable_behavior: after replacement, key is still 'child' and value is new number

operation_metadata.description: Create object and add item via cJSON_AddItemToObjectCS (case-sensitive insert, mapped to Object::insert)
operation_metadata.normalization: Object::insert with case-sensitive key (Rust always case-sensitive)

input.case: create object, add number via cJSON_AddItemToObjectCS (case-sensitive insert)

expected.observable_behavior: retrieved key is number with value 42

operation_metadata.description: Delete item from object by key using cJSON_DeleteItemFromObject and case-sensitive variant
operation_metadata.normalization: Object::remove returns Option<JsonValue>, source discards return. Same behavior for case-sensitive (Rust is case-sensitive).

input.case: create object, add key, delete it (both case-sensitive and insensitive variants)

expected.observable_behavior: after deletion, key retrieval returns None

operation_metadata.description: Check existence of a key in an object using cJSON_HasObjectItem, mapped to has_key
operation_metadata.normalization: JsonValue::has_key returns true if key exists in object, false otherwise (on non-object returns false)

input.case: create object, insert key, check has_key true for existing, false for missing, false on non-object

expected.observable_behavior: has_key returns correct boolean based on key existence and value type

operation_metadata.description: Pretty print a JSON object using cJSON_Print, mapped to pretty with spaces=2
operation_metadata.normalization: JsonValue::pretty(spaces) returns a formatted JSON string with newlines and indentation

input.case: create object with two keys, pretty print with spaces=2 and compare to expected string

expected.observable_behavior: pretty returns formatted JSON string with newlines and indentation

operation_metadata.description: Detach item from object by key; returns the removed item or None on failure (simulating NULL return for null pointers)
operation_metadata.normalization: Object::remove returns Some(value) if key exists, None if key missing or object empty. Source returns NULL for null object/string; Rust maps missing key to None.

input.case: create object, add item, detach by key, verify returned value and that key is removed

expected.observable_behavior: remove returns Some(value) for existing key; after removal key not found. Also missing key returns None.

operation_metadata.description: Detach item from object by key; returns the removed item or None on failure (simulating NULL return for null pointers)
operation_metadata.normalization: Object::remove returns Some(value) if key exists, None if key missing or object empty. Source returns NULL for null object/string; Rust maps missing key to None.

input.case: detach with missing key, empty object, empty key string

expected.observable_behavior: remove returns None for missing key, empty key, and on empty object

operation_metadata.description: Replace item in object case-sensitively; returns Ok(()) on success, Err on non-object (simulating false return for null pointer)
operation_metadata.normalization: JsonValue::insert overwrites value for existing key, returns Ok(()) on Object, Err on non-object. Source returns false for null object/string/newitem; Rust maps to error on non-object.

input.case: replace item in object case-sensitively, also test on non-object (null) returns error

expected.observable_behavior: JsonValue::insert on object with existing key replaces value Ok(); on non-object (Null) returns Err
